item.title: Two killed by train at Ullapara
item.description: Two young people died after being hit by a train when they were watching the blooming Shimul flowers in the Ghatina bridge area on the Ishwardi–Dhaka railway at Ullapara in Sirajganj on Thursday afternoon....

item.title: ME war risks deepening Myanmar crisis: UN
item.description: Myanmar’s spiralling rights and humanitarian crises risk getting ‘much worse’ as global attention focuses on the Middle East war and aid dwindles further, the top United Nations expert on the Asian country warned....

item.title: 108 Maoist leaders surrender in India
item.description: Indian officials said Wednesday that more than 100 Maoist rebels had surrendered, weeks ahead of New Delhi’s March 31 deadline to eliminate the insurgency entirely....

item.title: Arteta lauds Havertz on Leverkusen return
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta called Kai Havertz’s last-minute penalty against his former side Bayer Leverkusen in their Champions League last-16 tie on Wednesday an ‘extraordinary’ tale......

item.title: Chinese vice-foreign minister due in Dhaka early April
item.description: Chinese vice-foreign minister Sun Weidong is scheduled to visit Bangladesh in April 2-3 to lead a Chinese delegation to the 14th Bangladesh-China Diplomatic Consultations in Dhaka....

item.title: Nepal’s RSP party of Balendra Shah wins majority
item.description: Nepal’s centrist RSP party of rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah won a majority in parliament with 182 seats, Election Commission results showed Thursday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: 11 illegal brick kilns demolished in Bagerhat
item.description: A Bagerhat mobile court demolished 11 illegal brick kilns at Bagerhat Sadar in the district on Thursday....

item.title: 50 slaps of shoe, Tk 5000 fine imposed for rape attempt in Patuakhali
item.description: A 35-year-old van driver named Ripon was sentenced to 50 shoe slaps and fined Tk 5,000 for attempting to rape a class IV student of Dashkani government primary school at Kalapara upazila in Patuakhali district....

item.title: Mustafiz set to become first Bangladeshi to play in the Hundred
item.description: Mustafizur Rahman is set to become the first Bangladeshi to feature in the United Kingdom’s franchise-based league, The Hundred, as he was bought by Birmingham Phoenix at the inaugural auction of the league at the Piccadilly Lights in London on Thursday. ...

item.title: BMU observes World Kidney Day 2026
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University observed World Kidney Day 2026 at the university in Dhaka on Thursday....News analysis reports

item.title: E-health card to be launched by June: health minister
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain has expressed optimism that the national e-health card will be launched by June this year to make the country’s healthcare system stronger, more integrated and more effective....

item.title: Over 50 houses destroyed in nor’wester
item.description: In Lalmonirhat, a storm that lasted just 10 minutes destroyed more than 50 houses and shops in the Teesta Char area in early hours of Thursday....

item.title: DU teacher Jamal Uddin denied bail in anti-terrorism case
item.description: An additional chief metropolitan magistrate court in Dhaka on Thursday denied bail to Dhaka University professor AKM Jamal Uddin in a case filed under the Anti-Terrorism Act with the capital’s Paltan police station....

item.title: EKUSHEY BOOK FAIR: Few titles on drama
item.description: A few new titles on drama and related topics were published at the ongoing Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Dhaka seeks safe passage thru Hormuz Strait from Tehran
item.description: Bangladesh has sought safe passage for its vessels with energy items through the Strait of Hormuz from Iran now at war with the United States and Israel....

item.title: Ahmed Azam Khan sworn in as minister
item.description: Advocate Ahmed Azam Khan, vice-chairman of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and member of the parliament from Tangail 8, Basail-Sakhipur, was sworn in as a minister on Thursday....

item.title: Iran vows to keep Hormuz Strait shut
item.description: Iran’s new supreme leader ordered the vital Strait of Hormuz oil shipping lane to remain closed on Thursday, while US president Donald Trump said stopping the Islamic republic’s ‘evil empire’ was more important than crude prices....

item.title: Iran govt not at risk of collapse: US intel
item.description: US intelligence indicates that Iran’s leadership is still largely intact and is not at risk of collapse any time soon after nearly two weeks of relentless US and Israeli bombardment, according to three sources familiar with the matter who spoke to Reuters, reports......Geographic Reference

item.title: Change is constant, strategy should be, too
item.description: THE world is changing at an unprecedented pace. Societies are undergoing major shifts, from ageing populations in the Global North to a youth surge in the Global South. The fourth industrial revolution is accelerating automation, platform economies and the growing influence of artificial intelligence is reshaping labour markets and redefining human-machine......

item.title: Real estate factor for non-tariff insurance
item.description: THE non-life insurance market in Bangladesh currently comprises a number of companies that significantly exceeds prevailing market demand. This imbalance has intensified competition within the sector, and in some instances has led to practices that may be considered irregular or inconsistent with ethical standards. Such behaviour has contributed to a negative public......

item.title: How US-Israel war on Iran shatters global trust
item.description: THE unfolding war against Iran, driven, in the view of many observers, by the strategic calculations of US president Donald Trump and Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu, has become a defining moment in the collapse of global trust in western diplomacy. What was presented as negotiation, de-escalation and dialogue now appears as a smokescreen for a.....

item.title: Humanising education
item.description: WE ARE often told that the future of Bangladesh lies in digital connectivity. In the rush to modernise, the country has become increasingly fascinated with devices in classrooms. Tablets, laptops and smart screens are frequently presented as symbols of educational progress. Yet this technological enthusiasm risks masking a deeper and older problem. By focusing heavily......

item.title: Dignitaries attend first sitting of 13th JS
item.description: The first sitting of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad on Thursday drew a large number of invited guests, including family members of prime minister Tarique Rahman, diplomats and July uprising participants....

item.title: Trust, ethics and accountability
item.description: IN A modern state, public confidence in the judicial process is not merely an administrative necessity — it constitutes one of the principal pillars of the nation’s moral foundation. Courts are not just venues for enforcing the law; they are arenas where public trust is most rigorously tested. When citizens approach the judiciary, they seek not only a verdict but also the......

item.title: Coordinated, sustained measures must to contain dengue
item.description: THE prime minister’s call for a nationwide cleanliness campaign to head off dengue and chikungunya is welcome at a time when mosquito-borne diseases continue to threaten public health. The announcement of a weekly cleanliness drive beginning on March 14, along with the appeal to citizens and public representatives to maintain hygienic surrounding, reflects an......

item.title: Shahabuddin hails 2024 uprising for ousting fascists
item.description: President Mohammed Shahabuddin on Thursday, while addressing the maiden session of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad, credited the participants of the July 2024 uprising with the ousting of the then ‘fascist’ government on August 5, 2024....

item.title: Retail profiteering should not hurt boro farming
item.description: THE fuel crisis that has loomed large owing to the US-Israel war on Iran and subsequent disruption in the passage of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz appears to have caused concern for rice farming. Farmers in several districts keep, as New Age reported on March 12, struggling to get diesel for the irrigation of boro rice. The Department of Agricultural Extension......

item.title: ACC work stalls in leadership vacuum
item.description: Activities of the Anti-Corruption Commission have slowed down as the agency has been running without the chair and commissioners for more than a week after their resignation....

item.title: Lightning kills 2 farmers in Naogaon
item.description: Two farmers were killed by lightning strikes in separate incidents at Dhamoirhat and Manda upazilas in Naogaon on Thursday....

item.title: Man stabs brother to death in Moulvibazar
item.description: A man stabbed his teenage brother to death in Moulvibazar on Wednesday evening for scolding his son by the victim....

item.title: Haribhanga buds blanket Rangpur orchards ahead of mango season
item.description: Mango orchards in Rangpur are now awash with buds, lifting the spirits of farmers who are hoping for a bumper harvest of the region’s famed Haribhanga variety mango this season....

item.title: British Council joins global edn ventures
item.description: The British Council has been formally recognised by UNESCO as a new member of two key global education initiatives — the Global Education Coalition and the International Task Force on Teachers for Education 2030....

item.title: Tigers eye series win over Pakistan
item.description: Bangladesh will be targeting their second bilateral series win over Pakistan when the two sides face off in the second of the three-match ODI series at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur today......

item.title: Truck carrying oil recovered from river at Paturia
item.description: Bangladesh Inland Water Transport Authority on Thursday completed the recovery of an oil-laden truck that had fallen into river at Paturia in Manikganj....

item.title: Hamza to miss nat’l camp initial phase
item.description: Leicester City midfielder Hamza Choudhury will miss the Bangladesh national football team’s training camp that starts at the Kings Arena in the city on March 16......

item.title: Rajshahi BNP protests at NESCO office relocation to Bogura
item.description: Leaders and activists of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party and its associated bodies on Thursday formed a human chain in Rajshahi protesting against the move to relocate the headquarters of the Northern Electricity Supply Company Limited from the city to Bogura....

item.title: Dhaka’s rail link with northern, southern parts resumes after 6hrs
Rishad goes unsold at Hundred auction
item.description: Bangladesh leg-spinner Rishad Hossain was unsold in the inaugural auction of the United Kingdom’s franchise-based league, The Hundred, at the Piccadilly Lights in London on Thursday......

item.title: Man sent to jail for assaulting mother in Khulna
item.description: A Khulna court on Thursday sent a man, Masum Billah, 30, to jail in a case filed by his mother accusing him of assaulting her and forcing her out of the house....

item.title: Real batter City as PSG sink Chelsea in Champions League
item.description: Federico Valverde scored an unlikely hat-trick as Real Madrid trounced Manchester City 3-0 in the first leg of their Champions League last-16 tie Wednesday, while holders Paris Saint-Germain beat Chelsea 5-2......

item.title: JU suspends student for cyber-bullying
item.description: The authorities of Jahangirnagar University have suspended a student after finding evidence of cyber-bullying and abusive online conduct against another student, according to an official order issued on Thursday....

item.title: Transformer blast injures 3 in Gazipur
item.description: Three people suffered burn injuries after an electrical transformer exploded at Sripur in Gazipur on Wednesday night, triggering a fire that spread to a nearby market....

item.title: Rosenior defends Jorgensen after keeper gaffe costs Chelsea against PSG
item.description: Chelsea coach Liam Rosenior defended Filip Jorgensen after the Danish goalkeeper’s mistake sparked a late collapse which saw his side lose 5-2 to a rampant Paris Saint-Germain in the first leg of their Champions League last-16 tie on Wednesday......

item.title: Maldivian resumes Male-Dhaka flights
item.description: Maldivian, the national airline of the Maldives, resumed its Male-Dhaka-Male service on Thursday with a ceremonial water cannon salute at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka, restoring direct connectivity between the two South Asian neighbours......

item.title: Bangladesh mission in Doha plans special flight for expatriates
item.description: Bangladesh’s embassy in Doha is working with Qatari authorities and Qatar Airways to arrange a special flight to help Bangladeshi nationals stranded in Qatar return home amid ongoing tensions in the Gulf region....
